project.name: @tourism/api
project.description: NestJS 11 REST API for a Lily-style tourism booking platform (part of an Nx + pnpm monorepo), backed by Prisma/Postgres (Supabase) with Stripe and PayPal payments, Cloudinary media, Resend email, and pg-boss background jobs. Note: this project has over 100 source files; consider scoping analysis to a subdirectory for faster results.

nodes.name: current-user.decorator.ts
nodes.summary: Parameter decorators (CurrentUser, SupabaseIdentity) that extract the authenticated local user and the raw Supabase identity from the request object in NestJS handlers.

nodes.name: roles.decorator.ts
nodes.summary: Defines the @Roles metadata decorator that tags controllers/handlers with the roles required for access, consumed by RolesGuard.

nodes.name: roles.guard.ts
nodes.summary: NestJS guard enforcing role-based access control by reading @Roles metadata via the Reflector and checking it against the authenticated user's role.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses NestJS Reflector.getAllAndOverride to merge handler- and class-level role metadata.

nodes.name: authenticated-request.ts
nodes.summary: Type definitions describing the authenticated Express request shape and the Supabase auth identity attached to it after JWT verification.

nodes.name: auth.controller.ts
nodes.summary: Auth controller exposing customer and admin sync endpoints that mirror a Supabase-authenticated identity into the local user table.

nodes.name: auth.module.ts
nodes.summary: NestJS module wiring the AuthController and AuthService together for the authentication feature.

nodes.name: auth.service.ts
nodes.summary: Upserts Supabase-authenticated identities into the local user table, resolving admin role from an environment allowlist and keeping profile fields in sync.

nodes.name: sync-user.dto.ts
nodes.summary: Validated DTO carrying optional profile fields (fullName, phone) supplied when syncing a Supabase user to the local database.

nodes.name: admin-uploads.controller.ts
nodes.summary: Admin-only controller that returns signed Cloudinary upload parameters for authenticated direct-to-Cloudinary uploads.

nodes.name: create-signed-upload-url.dto.ts
nodes.summary: Validated DTO for a signed-upload request specifying the upload purpose, filename, and content type.

nodes.name: uploads.module.ts
nodes.summary: NestJS module bundling the uploads service and admin uploads controller.

nodes.name: uploads.service.ts
nodes.summary: Generates signed Cloudinary upload parameters, resolving resource type and folder per purpose, enforcing an extension/format allowlist, and deriving stable public IDs.
nodes.languageNotes: Reads Cloudinary credentials lazily in onModuleInit via ConfigService.getOrThrow and signs params with the Cloudinary SDK.

nodes.name: admin-users.controller.ts
nodes.summary: Admin controller for user management, exposing list, detail, role-change, and delete endpoints guarded by the caller's admin identity.

nodes.name: admin-users.service.ts
nodes.summary: Business logic for admin user administration: paginated search, detail with booking/review/wishlist counts, role changes with last-admin protection, and cascading user deletion.

nodes.name: admin-user.dto.ts
nodes.summary: Response and request DTOs for admin user management, including list item, paginated list, detail with counts, role-change, and deleted-user shapes.

nodes.name: list-admin-users-query.dto.ts
nodes.summary: Query DTO for the admin user listing endpoint, carrying pagination, role filter, and free-text search parameters.

nodes.name: set-avatar.dto.ts
nodes.summary: Validated DTO describing a Cloudinary avatar asset (publicId, format, width, height) to persist for a user.

nodes.name: sign-avatar.dto.ts
nodes.summary: Validated DTO for requesting a signed avatar upload, carrying filename and content type.

nodes.name: update-me.dto.ts
nodes.summary: Validated DTO for a user updating their own profile fields (fullName, phone).

nodes.name: user.dto.ts
nodes.summary: Public User response DTO exposing identity, profile, role, avatar, and timestamp fields.

nodes.name: users.controller.ts
nodes.summary: User self-service controller exposing profile read/update, avatar sign/set/clear, and self-deletion endpoints for the authenticated user.

nodes.name: users.module.ts
nodes.summary: NestJS module wiring the users and admin-users controllers/services and importing the media and uploads modules.

nodes.name: users.service.ts
nodes.summary: User profile service handling profile read/update, avatar attach/sync/clear via the media service, and self-deletion including removal of the Supabase auth user.
nodes.languageNotes: Deletes the remote Supabase auth user through a direct fetch to the admin API and wraps local deletion in a Prisma $transaction.

nodes.name: RolesGuard
nodes.summary: CanActivate guard that resolves required roles from route metadata and authorizes the request only if the authenticated user holds a matching role.

nodes.name: AuthController
nodes.summary: Controller exposing syncCustomer and syncAdmin endpoints that reconcile a Supabase identity into the local user record.

nodes.name: AuthService
nodes.summary: Service that upserts Supabase-authenticated users, applying an admin allowlist and keeping profile fields synchronized in the local database.

nodes.name: AdminUploadsController
nodes.summary: Admin controller whose sign endpoint returns signed Cloudinary upload parameters from the uploads service.

nodes.name: UploadsService
nodes.summary: Service generating signed Cloudinary upload params, selecting resource type/folder per purpose, enforcing a format allowlist, and deriving deterministic public IDs.

nodes.name: AdminUsersController
nodes.summary: Admin controller delegating user list, detail, role-change, and delete operations to AdminUsersService while passing the calling admin's identity.

nodes.name: AdminUsersService
nodes.summary: Service implementing admin user administration: paginated search, detail with aggregate counts, guarded role changes, and cascading user + media + Supabase deletion.

nodes.name: UsersController
nodes.summary: Self-service user controller handling profile read/update, avatar sign/set/clear, and account self-deletion for the authenticated caller.

nodes.name: UsersService
nodes.summary: Service managing user profile updates, avatar lifecycle via the media service, and self-deletion including remote Supabase auth user removal.

nodes.name: app.controller.ts
nodes.summary: Root controller exposing the base data route and a public /health endpoint that pings the database via a raw Prisma query.

nodes.name: AppController
nodes.summary: NestJS controller delegating to AppService for base data and running a DB liveness check for the /health probe.

nodes.name: app.module.ts
nodes.summary: Root application module wiring configuration, global guards/filters/interceptors, and every feature module (auth, bookings, tours, newsletter, admin-stats, etc.).
nodes.languageNotes: NestJS module aggregates 26 imports and registers app-wide providers (SupabaseJwtGuard, RolesGuard, HttpExceptionFilter, TransformInterceptor) via APP_* tokens.

nodes.name: app.service.ts
nodes.summary: Trivial root service returning a static greeting payload for the base route.

nodes.name: public.decorator.ts
nodes.summary: Defines the @Public() decorator and IS_PUBLIC_KEY metadata used by the JWT guard to whitelist unauthenticated routes.

nodes.name: http-exception.filter.ts
nodes.summary: Global exception filter that normalizes thrown errors into a consistent JSON error envelope, mapping HTTP status to error codes and reporting 5xx to Sentry.

nodes.name: HttpExceptionFilter
nodes.summary: Catches all exceptions, builds a uniform error response body, logs and captures unexpected 5xx errors to Sentry.

nodes.name: supabase-jwt.guard.ts
nodes.summary: Global authentication guard that verifies Supabase JWTs (remote JWKS for RS256, shared HS256 secret), honors @Public() routes, and attaches the mirrored local user to the request.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses jose (createRemoteJWKSet / jwtVerify / decodeProtectedHeader) to support both asymmetric JWKS and symmetric HS256 verification paths.

nodes.name: SupabaseJwtGuard
nodes.summary: CanActivate guard extracting the bearer token, verifying it against Supabase keys, and resolving the authenticated user via Prisma before allowing the request.

nodes.name: index.ts
nodes.summary: Barrel re-exporting the environment validation schema used by the NestJS ConfigModule.

nodes.name: main.ts
nodes.summary: Application bootstrap: initializes Sentry, creates the Nest app, wires Stripe raw-body middleware, helmet, CORS, global validation pipe, Swagger docs, and starts the HTTP listener.

nodes.name: bootstrap
nodes.summary: Async entry function that configures security middleware, the global validation pipe, Swagger/OpenAPI, and starts the server on the configured port.

nodes.name: admin-stats.controller.ts
nodes.summary: Admin-only controller exposing the dashboard statistics endpoint, guarded by the ADMIN role.

nodes.name: AdminStatsController
nodes.summary: Controller returning the aggregated admin dashboard via AdminStatsService.getDashboard.

nodes.name: admin-stats.module.ts
nodes.summary: NestJS module bundling the admin statistics controller and service.

nodes.name: admin-stats.service.ts
nodes.summary: Computes the admin dashboard: booking/revenue aggregates, top tours by revenue/rating/wishlist, monthly and daily trends, and pending review/enquiry counts via parallel Prisma queries.
nodes.languageNotes: Runs a batch of grouped/aggregate Prisma queries plus raw SQL trend queries in parallel with Promise.all, then reshapes results into the response DTO.

nodes.name: AdminStatsService
nodes.summary: Service whose getDashboard method assembles all dashboard metrics from grouped aggregates, raw SQL trend queries, and tour lookups.

nodes.name: admin-stats-response.dto.ts
nodes.summary: Swagger-annotated response DTOs describing the admin dashboard shape (overview, top-tour breakdowns, monthly/daily trends, pending counts).

nodes.name: admin-newsletter.controller.ts
nodes.summary: Admin-only controller listing newsletter subscribers with pagination and search, guarded by the ADMIN role.

nodes.name: AdminNewsletterController
nodes.summary: Controller returning a paginated subscriber list via NewsletterService.findAllForAdmin.

nodes.name: list-subscribers-query.dto.ts
nodes.summary: Query DTO validating pagination (page/pageSize) and optional search for the admin subscriber list.

nodes.name: subscribe.dto.ts
nodes.summary: Public subscribe request DTO (email, source, honeypot website field) and the acknowledgement response DTO.

nodes.name: newsletter.controller.ts
nodes.summary: Public throttled controller accepting newsletter subscriptions, silently dropping honeypot-triggered spam submissions.

nodes.name: NewsletterController
nodes.summary: Controller whose subscribe handler filters honeypot submissions and delegates persistence to NewsletterService.

nodes.name: newsletter.module.ts
nodes.summary: NestJS module wiring the public and admin newsletter controllers with the newsletter service and rate limiting.

nodes.name: newsletter.service.ts
nodes.summary: Newsletter business logic: idempotent subscriber upsert (silent dedupe) and paginated/searchable admin listing via Prisma.

nodes.name: NewsletterService
nodes.summary: Service exposing subscribe (email-normalizing upsert) and findAllForAdmin (paginated, searchable subscriber query).

nodes.name: prisma.module.ts
nodes.summary: Global NestJS module providing and exporting the shared PrismaService.

nodes.name: prisma.service.ts
nodes.summary: PrismaClient wrapper using the PrismaPg driver adapter, connecting on module init and disconnecting on destroy for lifecycle-managed DB access.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses the PrismaPg driver adapter required by the Supabase transaction pooler; implements OnModuleInit/OnModuleDestroy for connection lifecycle.

nodes.name: PrismaService
nodes.summary: Lifecycle-managed PrismaClient extension that connects on init and disconnects on destroy.

nodes.name: app.e2e.ts
nodes.summary: End-to-end test suite booting the full Nest app, minting HS256 Supabase JWTs, and exercising auth/role-gated routes as customer and admin.

nodes.name: skip-transform.decorator.ts
nodes.summary: Declares the @SkipTransform decorator and its metadata key, letting a route opt out of the global response-envelope interceptor (e.g. raw webhook responses).

nodes.name: transform.interceptor.ts
nodes.summary: Global NestJS interceptor that wraps successful controller responses in the standard success envelope, honoring the @SkipTransform metadata to bypass wrapping.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses RxJS map operator on the CallHandler stream and Reflector metadata lookup to conditionally transform.

nodes.name: TransformInterceptor
nodes.summary: Implements NestInterceptor to reshape handler output into the project's success envelope unless the handler or controller is marked @SkipTransform.

nodes.name: admin-bookings.controller.ts
nodes.summary: Admin-only REST controller exposing booking list, detail, and refund endpoints, delegating to BookingsService and guarded by role decorators.

nodes.name: AdminBookingsController
nodes.summary: Handles admin booking routes (paginated list, detail-by-code, and refund execution) by forwarding to BookingsService.

nodes.name: booking-code.ts
nodes.summary: Pure helper that mints a human-readable, collision-resistant booking reference code from cryptographic random bytes.

nodes.name: mintBookingCode
nodes.summary: Generates a random booking code using crypto randomBytes, encoding it into a fixed-length uppercase reference.

nodes.name: bookings.controller.ts
nodes.summary: Customer-facing REST controller for the booking lifecycle: create, list own, cancel pending, start checkout, capture PayPal, and view detail.

nodes.name: BookingsController
nodes.summary: Exposes the authenticated user's booking endpoints and delegates all business logic to BookingsService, with a requireUser guard helper.

nodes.name: bookings.module.ts
nodes.summary: NestJS module wiring the bookings controllers and service together and importing the PaymentsModule for checkout/refund collaboration.

nodes.name: bookings.service.ts
nodes.summary: Core booking domain service: creates bookings with seat/pricing checks, starts Stripe/PayPal checkout, captures PayPal, cancels pending, executes admin refunds, and reads own/admin booking views.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses Prisma raw SQL ($queryRaw + Prisma.sql) for atomic seat-claim/refund transitions and Decimal arithmetic for money.

nodes.name: toAdminBookingDetail
nodes.summary: Maps a richly-included Prisma booking record into the AdminBookingDetailDto, serializing dates, decimals, and nested customer/departure/cancellation data.

nodes.name: BookingsService
nodes.summary: Injectable service encapsulating the full booking money path — creation, checkout session start, PayPal capture, cancellation, admin refund, and owner/admin queries.

nodes.name: admin-booking-detail.dto.ts
nodes.summary: Swagger-annotated DTO hierarchy describing the admin booking-detail response, including customer, departure, refund, payment-event, and cancellation-request sub-shapes.

nodes.name: AdminBookingDetailDto
nodes.summary: Top-level DTO for the admin booking-detail endpoint, extending the base booking shape with refund audit, customer, payment events, and other-bookings context.

nodes.name: booking.dto.ts
nodes.summary: Base booking response DTO plus tour/departure reference shapes shared across customer and admin booking endpoints.

nodes.name: BookingDto
nodes.summary: Canonical booking DTO carrying status, party sizes, pricing, contact info, tour/departure refs, and optional cancellation-request summary.

nodes.name: checkout-session.dto.ts
nodes.summary: Small DTO returned when starting checkout, carrying the provider checkout URL, booking code, and status.

nodes.name: create-booking.dto.ts
nodes.summary: Request DTO with class-validator rules for creating a booking: tour slug, departure, party sizes, payment provider, and contact details.

nodes.name: CreateBookingDto
nodes.summary: Validated create-booking payload constraining party sizes, contact fields, and the chosen payment provider.

nodes.name: list-admin-bookings-query.dto.ts
nodes.summary: Query DTO for the admin bookings list, defining pagination, status/search filters, and tour/departure/user scoping with validation.

nodes.name: ListAdminBookingsQueryDto
nodes.summary: Validated admin booking-list query parameters covering page/pageSize, status, free-text search, and entity filters.

nodes.name: paginated-bookings.dto.ts
nodes.summary: Envelope DTO pairing an array of BookingDto items with shared page-meta pagination metadata.

nodes.name: refund-booking.dto.ts
nodes.summary: Request DTO for an admin refund, carrying an optional partial amount and a reason for the refund.

nodes.name: refund-amount.ts
nodes.summary: Pure helper that classifies a refund request against the booking total as full or partial using Decimal comparison.

nodes.name: classifyRefund
nodes.summary: Given a booking total and requested refund amount, returns whether the refund is full or partial and the effective Decimal amount.

nodes.name: money.ts
nodes.summary: Currency conversion helpers translating Decimal amounts into Stripe minor units and PayPal string amounts, with zero-decimal currency handling.

nodes.name: toStripeMinorUnits
nodes.summary: Converts a Decimal amount to Stripe's integer minor-unit representation, respecting zero-decimal currencies.

nodes.name: toPayPalAmount
nodes.summary: Formats a Decimal amount into the fixed-decimal string PayPal expects, respecting zero-decimal currencies.

nodes.name: payments.controller.ts
nodes.summary: Public webhook controller receiving raw Stripe and PayPal payloads and forwarding them to PaymentsService for signature-verified processing.

nodes.name: PaymentsController
nodes.summary: Handles the Stripe and PayPal webhook endpoints, passing the raw request body and signature headers to PaymentsService.

nodes.name: payments.module.ts
nodes.summary: NestJS module bundling the payments controller with the Stripe, PayPal, and payments services and exporting them for other modules.

nodes.name: payments.service.ts
nodes.summary: Webhook processing service that idempotently records payment events and reconciles Stripe checkout and PayPal capture outcomes, claiming seats or refunding overbooked bookings.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses a paymentEvent table with unique-constraint idempotency and raw SQL seat-claim to guard against overbooking under concurrency.

nodes.name: PaymentsService
nodes.summary: Verifies and dispatches Stripe/PayPal webhook events, atomically claims seats for paid bookings, expires abandoned checkouts, and refunds overbooked captures.

nodes.name: paypal.service.ts
nodes.summary: PayPal integration service creating and capturing orders, refunding captures, verifying webhook signatures, and caching OAuth access tokens.
nodes.languageNotes: Caches an OAuth bearer token with expiry and uses the PayPal SDK plus raw fetch for webhook-signature verification.

nodes.name: PayPalService
nodes.summary: Encapsulates PayPal order lifecycle (create/capture/refund), idempotent already-captured handling, webhook signature verification, and cached access-token retrieval.

nodes.name: stripe.service.ts
nodes.summary: Stripe integration service that lazily initializes the SDK client, creates Checkout sessions, verifies webhook events, and issues refunds.

nodes.name: StripeService
nodes.summary: Wraps the Stripe SDK to create Checkout sessions, construct verified webhook events, and issue refunds with documented reason validation.

nodes.name: page-meta.dto.ts
nodes.summary: Shared pagination metadata DTO (page, pageSize, total, totalPages) reused by every paginated response envelope across modules.

nodes.name: admin-media.dto.ts
nodes.summary: Admin media response DTOs covering media assets, paginated lists, Cloudinary garbage rows, reconcile results, and deletion acknowledgements.

nodes.name: subscriber.dto.ts
nodes.summary: Newsletter subscriber DTO plus its paginated wrapper used by the admin subscribers listing.

nodes.name: admin-reviews.controller.ts
nodes.summary: Admin-only reviews controller exposing list, moderate, feature, delete, and curated-create endpoints, delegating to ReviewsService.

nodes.name: admin-review.dto.ts
nodes.summary: Admin review response DTO with tour/author/booking context and its paginated wrapper for the moderation dashboard.

nodes.name: create-curated-review.dto.ts
nodes.summary: Validated request DTO for admins to author curated (editorial) reviews with author name, location, rating, and body.

nodes.name: create-review.dto.ts
nodes.summary: Validated request DTO for a customer to submit a review tied to a booking code with rating, title, and body.

nodes.name: feature-review.dto.ts
nodes.summary: Minimal request DTO carrying the isFeatured boolean for toggling a review's featured status.

nodes.name: featured-review.dto.ts
nodes.summary: Public featured-review response DTO and its collection wrapper used on marketing surfaces.

nodes.name: list-admin-reviews-query.dto.ts
nodes.summary: Query DTO for the admin reviews listing with pagination and an isApproved moderation filter.

nodes.name: list-reviews-query.dto.ts
nodes.summary: Query DTO providing page and pageSize parameters for the public per-tour reviews listing.

nodes.name: moderate-review.dto.ts
nodes.summary: Minimal request DTO carrying the isApproved boolean for approving or rejecting a review in moderation.

nodes.name: public-review.dto.ts
nodes.summary: Public review response DTOs including reviewer identity, review body, average-rating page meta, and the paginated wrapper.

nodes.name: review.dto.ts
nodes.summary: Base review response DTO mirroring the persisted review record (tour, user, booking, rating, approval, timestamps).

nodes.name: public-reviews.controller.ts
nodes.summary: Public unauthenticated controller listing approved reviews for a given tour with pagination and average rating.

nodes.name: reviews.controller.ts
nodes.summary: Customer-facing reviews controller exposing the featured-reviews feed and authenticated review creation tied to the current user.

nodes.name: reviews.module.ts
nodes.summary: NestJS module wiring the three reviews controllers and ReviewsService into the application's dependency-injection graph.

nodes.name: reviews.service.ts
nodes.summary: Core reviews business logic: customer review creation with booking validation, per-tour approved listings, featured feed, and the full admin moderation/curation lifecycle with transactional outbox events.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses Prisma $transaction with an outbox createMany to atomically moderate a review and enqueue downstream events; parallel reads via Promise.all to avoid the pooler's single-connection transaction limit.

nodes.name: ReviewsService
nodes.summary: Injectable service encapsulating review creation, retrieval, moderation, featuring, curation, and deletion against Prisma.

nodes.name: AdminReviewsController
nodes.summary: Admin-guarded controller routing list, moderate, feature, delete, and curated-create requests to ReviewsService.

nodes.name: ReviewsController
nodes.summary: Customer controller serving the featured-reviews feed and authenticated per-user review creation.

nodes.name: PublicReviewsController
nodes.summary: Public controller listing approved reviews for a tour.

nodes.name: AdminMediaAssetDto
nodes.summary: Rich admin media asset DTO describing a Cloudinary asset with owner context, dimensions, format, and role.

nodes.name: AdminReviewDto
nodes.summary: Admin review DTO joining review fields with tour, author, booking, source, and approval metadata for moderation.

nodes.name: PublicReviewDto
nodes.summary: Public-facing review DTO exposing rating, title, body, timestamp, and embedded reviewer identity.

nodes.name: FeaturedReviewDto
nodes.summary: Featured review DTO surfacing rating, body, and author attribution for marketing highlight sections.

nodes.name: SubscriberDto
nodes.summary: Newsletter subscriber DTO with id, email, source, and subscription timestamp.

nodes.name: cloudinary-url.ts
nodes.summary: Pure helper that assembles Cloudinary delivery URLs from a cloud name and stored media asset, passing through already-absolute URLs unchanged.

nodes.name: buildCloudinaryUrl
nodes.summary: Builds a fully-qualified Cloudinary URL for a media asset, short-circuiting when the asset already holds an absolute URL.

nodes.name: email.module.ts
nodes.summary: NestJS module that provides and exports EmailService for transactional email delivery across the app.

nodes.name: email.service.ts
nodes.summary: Resend-backed transactional email service that renders and dispatches booking, refund, review, and enquiry notification emails.
nodes.languageNotes: Implements OnModuleInit to lazily resolve Resend config and the from-address at bootstrap.

nodes.name: EmailService
nodes.summary: Wraps the Resend client to send four transactional email types, each rendered from a shared template module then dispatched with logging.

nodes.name: email.templates.ts
nodes.summary: Pure render functions producing subject/text/html bodies for booking-confirmation, refund, review-approved, and enquiry-received emails.
nodes.languageNotes: Text bodies are assembled as line arrays joined with newlines, then escaped into simple paragraph HTML.

nodes.name: renderBookingConfirmation
nodes.summary: Renders the booking-confirmation email (subject/text/html) from traveler, tour, date, and payment variables.

nodes.name: renderBookingRefunded
nodes.summary: Renders the booking-refunded email body notifying the traveler of the refunded amount and expected timeline.

nodes.name: renderReviewApproved
nodes.summary: Renders the review-approved email, including a star-rating glyph string derived from the numeric rating.

nodes.name: renderEnquiryReceived
nodes.summary: Renders the enquiry-received acknowledgement email echoing the visitor's message back to them.

nodes.name: admin-outbox.controller.ts
nodes.summary: Admin REST controller exposing paginated listing and manual retry of outbox (deferred email) rows.

nodes.name: AdminOutboxController
nodes.summary: Role-guarded controller delegating outbox list and retry requests to AdminOutboxService.

nodes.name: admin-outbox.service.ts
nodes.summary: Service backing the admin outbox view: paginated Prisma queries over outbox rows plus a retry action that resets a failed row for redelivery.

nodes.name: AdminOutboxService
nodes.summary: Reads and maps outbox rows for the admin queue and re-queues a failed row by resetting its status and attempt counters.

nodes.name: admin-outbox.dto.ts
nodes.summary: DTOs for the admin outbox endpoints: list query params, a serialized outbox row, and the paginated response envelope.

nodes.name: jobs.module.ts
nodes.summary: NestJS module wiring the background-jobs stack: pg-boss runner, outbox drain, maintenance tasks, and the admin outbox controller.

nodes.name: jobs.service.ts
nodes.summary: pg-boss lifecycle service that starts the queue, registers workers, and schedules the outbox drain, abandoned-booking cancellation, and media reconciliation cron jobs.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses OnModuleInit/OnModuleDestroy to bind the pg-boss instance lifecycle to the Nest module lifecycle; dynamic import of pg-boss at init.

nodes.name: JobsService
nodes.summary: Boots pg-boss, creates queues, and schedules recurring workers that drain the outbox and run maintenance tasks, shutting the boss down on module destroy.

nodes.name: maintenance.service.ts
nodes.summary: Scheduled maintenance tasks: cancel long-abandoned pending bookings and reconcile orphaned media by destroying Cloudinary assets tracked in the garbage table.

nodes.name: MaintenanceService
nodes.summary: Bulk-cancels expired pending bookings and processes the media-garbage queue, deleting Cloudinary assets and clearing rows while tolerating per-item failures.

nodes.name: outbox.service.ts
nodes.summary: Transactional-outbox drainer that reads pending rows, dispatches the matching email via EmailService, and records success or bounded-retry failure state.
nodes.languageNotes: Implements the transactional outbox pattern with per-row status/attempt bookkeeping and hydration of email payloads from Prisma.

nodes.name: OutboxService
nodes.summary: Drains outbox rows and routes each to a typed sender (booking confirmation/refund, review approved, enquiry received) that hydrates data from Prisma and calls EmailService.

nodes.name: admin-media.controller.ts
nodes.summary: Admin REST controller for media management: list assets, list the garbage queue, trigger reconciliation, and delete an asset.

nodes.name: AdminMediaController
nodes.summary: Role-guarded controller delegating media list, garbage list, reconcile, and delete operations to AdminMediaService.

nodes.name: admin-media.module.ts
nodes.summary: NestJS module wiring the admin media controller and service, importing JobsModule for reconciliation support.

nodes.name: admin-media.service.ts
nodes.summary: Admin media data service: paginated/filtered asset listing with cross-entity owner resolution and search, safe asset deletion via a garbage queue, and reconcile delegation.
nodes.languageNotes: Resolves owner metadata across tours, destinations, posts, and users with batched Prisma lookups grouped by owner type.

nodes.name: AdminMediaService
nodes.summary: Lists media assets with search and owner enrichment, moves deleted assets into the media-garbage table within a transaction, and exposes garbage listing plus reconcile triggering.

nodes.name: cloudinary.service.ts
nodes.summary: Thin wrapper around the Cloudinary SDK, configured at bootstrap, exposing a destroy method to delete assets by public id.

nodes.name: CloudinaryService
nodes.summary: Configures the Cloudinary SDK from env at module init and wraps its uploader destroy call.

nodes.name: list-admin-media-query.dto.ts
nodes.summary: Query DTO validating admin media listing filters: pagination, owner type, media role, media type, and free-text search.

nodes.name: list-media-garbage-query.dto.ts
nodes.summary: Query DTO validating pagination params for the media-garbage listing endpoint.

nodes.name: media.module.ts
nodes.summary: NestJS module providing and exporting CloudinaryService and MediaService for media handling.

nodes.name: admin-destinations.controller.ts
nodes.summary: Admin REST controller for Destination CRUD (list, detail, create, update, set-media, remove) behind role-guarded routes.

nodes.name: destinations.controller.ts
nodes.summary: Public REST controller exposing destination list and slug detail reads for the storefront.

nodes.name: destinations.module.ts
nodes.summary: NestJS module wiring the destinations controllers, service, and MediaModule dependency.

nodes.name: destinations.service.ts
nodes.summary: Core service for Destination CRUD and search: public reads force isActive, admin reads see drafts, slug uniqueness and FK-restrict conflicts map to 409, and media is synced transactionally.
nodes.languageNotes: Maps Prisma error codes P2002 (unique) and P2003 (FK restrict) to ConflictException.

nodes.name: admin-destination-detail.dto.ts
nodes.summary: Admin detail DTOs: a destination enriched with the tours that use it plus a tours count.

nodes.name: create-destination.dto.ts
nodes.summary: Validated request DTO for creating a Destination (name, region, description, media, active flag).

nodes.name: destination.dto.ts
nodes.summary: Public response DTO shape for a Destination, including its resolved media items.

nodes.name: list-destinations-query.dto.ts
nodes.summary: Validated query DTO for paginated destination listing (search, region filter, pagination).

nodes.name: paginated-destinations.dto.ts
nodes.summary: Paginated response DTO wrapping destination items with page metadata.

nodes.name: update-destination.dto.ts
nodes.summary: Partial DTO for updating a Destination, derived from the create DTO.

nodes.name: media.dto.ts
nodes.summary: Shared media DTOs: MediaInputDto (write payload with Cloudinary publicId, role, type) and MediaItemDto (read shape with built delivery URL).

nodes.name: set-media.dto.ts
nodes.summary: Request DTO wrapping the full media array for a replace-all set-media operation.

nodes.name: media.service.ts
nodes.summary: Single owner of MediaAsset access: transactional replace-all sync, garbage-collection of dropped Cloudinary assets, and read-time delivery URL building for tours, destinations, and users.
nodes.languageNotes: Write methods accept a Prisma.TransactionClient to stay atomic with the owner mutation; the polymorphic (ownerType, ownerId) convention is centralized here.

nodes.name: wishlist-item.dto.ts
nodes.summary: Wishlist DTOs: a saved item plus a compact tour preview (title, slug, price, cover media) for the account saved list.

nodes.name: wishlist.controller.ts
nodes.summary: Authenticated REST controller to add, remove, and list the current user wishlist tours.

nodes.name: wishlist.module.ts
nodes.summary: NestJS module wiring the wishlist controller, service, and MediaModule dependency.

nodes.name: wishlist.service.ts
nodes.summary: Service managing a user wishlist: add/remove a tour and list saved tours enriched with cover media.

nodes.name: AdminDestinationsController
nodes.summary: Admin controller class handling destination list, detail, create, update, set-media, and remove endpoints.

nodes.name: DestinationsController
nodes.summary: Public controller class exposing destination list and slug-detail reads.

nodes.name: DestinationsService
nodes.summary: Service encapsulating Destination CRUD, public/admin read splits, slug normalization, and Prisma-error-to-HTTP mapping.

nodes.name: MediaService
nodes.summary: Central MediaAsset service handling transactional sync, GC of dropped assets, and delivery URL attachment for polymorphic owners.

nodes.name: WishlistController
nodes.summary: Controller class for adding, removing, and listing the current user wishlist tours.

nodes.name: WishlistService
nodes.summary: Service managing wishlist add/remove and media-enriched saved-tour listing for a user.

nodes.name: CreateDestinationDto
nodes.summary: Validated create payload for a Destination with class-validator decorators.

nodes.name: DestinationDto
nodes.summary: Public Destination response shape including resolved media items.

nodes.name: ListDestinationsQueryDto
nodes.summary: Validated query parameters for paginated destination search and filtering.

nodes.name: MediaInputDto
nodes.summary: Write payload DTO for a media asset (Cloudinary publicId, role, type, dimensions).

nodes.name: MediaItemDto
nodes.summary: Read-shape DTO for a media asset with the built delivery URL.

nodes.name: WishlistTourPreviewDto
nodes.summary: Compact tour preview embedded in a wishlist item (title, slug, price, cover media).

nodes.name: admin-tours.controller.ts
nodes.summary: Admin-only REST controller for tour management, exposing list/detail/create/update/set-media/delete endpoints guarded by the ADMIN role and delegating to ToursService.

nodes.name: AdminToursController
nodes.summary: NestJS controller handling admin CRUD over tours (including drafts), media replacement, and enriched admin detail, each documented with Swagger response codes.

nodes.name: admin-tour-detail.dto.ts
nodes.summary: Response DTOs for the admin tour detail view, adding operational fields (TourOpsDto) on top of the public TourDetailDto.

nodes.name: create-tour.dto.ts
nodes.summary: Large validated request DTO describing every field required to create a tour, including nested itinerary days, FAQs, policies, and category/destination references.

nodes.name: CreateTourDto
nodes.summary: class-validator/Swagger-decorated payload for tour creation, composing nested FAQ, itinerary-day and policy inputs alongside scalar tour attributes.

nodes.name: list-tours-query.dto.ts
nodes.summary: Query-string DTO for tour listing: free-text search, category/destination filters, pagination, and sort options shared by public and admin list endpoints.

nodes.name: ListToursQueryDto
nodes.summary: Validated, transformed query parameters (search, filters, page/limit, sort) driving the tours list pipeline.

nodes.name: tour-faq.input.ts
nodes.summary: Nested input DTO for a single tour FAQ (question/answer/order) embedded in create and update tour payloads.

nodes.name: tour-itinerary-day.input.ts
nodes.summary: Nested input DTO for one itinerary day (day number, title, description) used inside the create/update tour payloads.

nodes.name: tour-policy.input.ts
nodes.summary: Nested input DTO for a tour policy entry (kind/title/body) embedded in tour create and update requests.

nodes.name: paginated-tours.dto.ts
nodes.summary: Envelope DTO pairing a page of TourSummaryDto items with shared page metadata for list responses.

nodes.name: tour-relations.dto.ts
nodes.summary: Collection of relational sub-DTOs (category/destination refs, itinerary days, FAQs, policies) reused across tour summary and detail response shapes.

nodes.name: tour-summary.dto.ts
nodes.summary: Card-level tour response DTO carrying pricing, media, rating, relation refs, and next-departure availability for listing surfaces.

nodes.name: TourSummaryDto
nodes.summary: Swagger-annotated summary shape for a tour card, including next-departure date/seats-left and aggregated rating fields.

nodes.name: tour.dto.ts
nodes.summary: Full public tour detail DTO extending the summary shape with itinerary, FAQs, and policy relations.

nodes.name: update-tour.dto.ts
nodes.summary: Partial-update DTO derived from CreateTourDto via PartialType, making every tour field optional for PATCH.

nodes.name: next-departure.util.ts
nodes.summary: Pure, I/O-free helpers computing a tour's next open upcoming departure (date + seats left) for card availability, unit-testable in isolation.

nodes.name: nextDepartureInfo
nodes.summary: Builds the next-departure availability summary from the soonest open departure row, returning null fields when none exists and clamping seats-left at zero.

nodes.name: tours.controller.ts
nodes.summary: Public REST controller exposing tour list and detail endpoints (published-only) marked @Public and delegating to ToursService.

nodes.name: ToursController
nodes.summary: Public NestJS controller with list and detail-by-slug endpoints returning paginated summaries and full tour detail.

nodes.name: tours.module.ts
nodes.summary: NestJS module wiring the public and admin tour controllers with ToursService and importing the media module.

nodes.name: tours.service.ts
nodes.summary: Core tours service implementing public/admin list and detail reads, CRUD with slug/relation resolution, media sync, rating aggregation, and next-departure enrichment over Prisma.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses shared Prisma include constants (LIST_INCLUDE/DETAIL_INCLUDE) and $transaction only for low-concurrency admin writes to stay pooler-safe.

nodes.name: ToursService
nodes.summary: Injectable service orchestrating tour queries and mutations: filter/sort/paginate lists, enrich with media, ratings and next departure, resolve category/destination links, and enforce slug uniqueness and delete guards.

nodes.name: slugify.ts
nodes.summary: Shared helper that converts arbitrary strings into URL-safe slugs, used for generating post and content slugs across the API.

nodes.name: slugify
nodes.summary: Normalizes a string to a lowercase, hyphen-separated, URL-safe slug by stripping diacritics and non-alphanumeric characters.

nodes.name: admin-posts.controller.ts
nodes.summary: Admin-facing REST controller for blog posts exposing CRUD, tag listing, media set, and inline body-image registration endpoints, guarded by role decorators.
nodes.languageNotes: NestJS controller using @Roles/@CurrentUser decorators and DTO-typed request/response contracts.

nodes.name: AdminPostsController
nodes.summary: Handles admin post management routes (list, detail, create, update, set media, add body image, delete, tags), delegating persistence to PostsService.

nodes.name: admin-post-detail.dto.ts
nodes.summary: Response DTOs for the admin post-detail view, including author, related-tour, and the composed admin post detail shape.

nodes.name: create-post.dto.ts
nodes.summary: Validated request DTO describing the fields accepted when creating a blog post (title, body, tags, related tours, status, etc.).
nodes.languageNotes: class-validator decorators enforce request-body validation on incoming create payloads.

nodes.name: list-posts-query.dto.ts
nodes.summary: Validated query-string DTO for listing posts with pagination, search, tag, and status filter parameters.

nodes.name: paginated-posts.dto.ts
nodes.summary: Envelope DTO pairing an array of post items with page metadata for paginated post listings.

nodes.name: post-detail.dto.ts
nodes.summary: Public post-detail response DTO extending the base post shape with related tour summaries.

nodes.name: post-tag.dto.ts
nodes.summary: Tag DTOs for posts: a basic tag shape and a variant carrying an associated post count for tag listings.

nodes.name: post.dto.ts
nodes.summary: Core public post DTO and its embedded author shape, composing media and tag DTOs into the canonical post response.

nodes.name: register-body-image.dto.ts
nodes.summary: Request/response DTOs for registering an inline body image on a post, capturing the uploaded asset URL and metadata.

nodes.name: update-post.dto.ts
nodes.summary: Partial update DTO for posts derived from CreatePostDto, making all create fields optional for PATCH semantics.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses NestJS PartialType to derive an all-optional variant of CreatePostDto.

nodes.name: posts.controller.ts
nodes.summary: Public REST controller exposing read-only blog endpoints: list posts, list tags, and fetch a post by slug.
nodes.languageNotes: Routes marked @Public bypass auth guards for anonymous blog reading.

nodes.name: PostsController
nodes.summary: Serves public blog reads (paginated list, tag list, detail by slug) by delegating to PostsService.

nodes.name: posts.module.ts
nodes.summary: NestJS module wiring the public and admin post controllers with PostsService, importing the media and tours modules.

nodes.name: posts.service.ts
nodes.summary: Core posts business-logic service handling public and admin queries, CRUD, slug uniqueness, tag normalization, related-tour linking, media, and DTO hydration over Prisma.
nodes.languageNotes: Large NestJS provider that composes Prisma queries with MediaService/ToursService and hydrates rich DTOs; handles unique-constraint conflicts for slugs.

nodes.name: PostsService
nodes.summary: Encapsulates all post operations: public list/detail/tags, admin CRUD, slug conflict handling, tag and related-tour link management, media set/body-image registration, and DTO hydration.

nodes.name: admin-tour-categories.controller.ts
nodes.summary: Admin-only REST controller exposing full CRUD (list, detail, create, update, remove) over tour categories, guarded by the Roles decorator.

nodes.name: admin-tour-category-detail.dto.ts
nodes.summary: Response DTO for the admin category-detail endpoint, extending the base category shape with a nested list of associated tours.

nodes.name: create-tour-category.dto.ts
nodes.summary: Validated request DTO for creating a tour category (name, optional slug, description, order, isActive) using class-validator decorators.

nodes.name: list-tour-categories-query.dto.ts
nodes.summary: Query-string DTO for listing tour categories with pagination, search, active filter, and sort controls.

nodes.name: paginated-tour-categories.dto.ts
nodes.summary: Paginated response envelope wrapping a data array of TourCategoryDto with shared PageMetaDto metadata.

nodes.name: tour-category.dto.ts
nodes.summary: Base response DTO describing a tour category with identifiers, editorial fields, ordering, timestamps, and a computed tours count.

nodes.name: update-tour-category.dto.ts
nodes.summary: Partial update DTO derived from CreateTourCategoryDto, making all category fields optional for PATCH requests.

nodes.name: tour-categories.controller.ts
nodes.summary: Public REST controller exposing read-only category listing and detail-by-slug endpoints marked with the Public decorator.

nodes.name: tour-categories.module.ts
nodes.summary: NestJS module wiring the public and admin controllers together with the shared TourCategoriesService.

nodes.name: tour-categories.service.ts
nodes.summary: Core service implementing tour-category business logic: paginated public/admin listing, slug lookups, CRUD with slug normalization, and Prisma unique/foreign-key error translation.
nodes.languageNotes: Centralizes Prisma error-code handling (P2002 unique, P2003 foreign-key) into HTTP-friendly conflict/not-found exceptions.

nodes.name: AdminTourCategoriesController
nodes.summary: Admin controller handling list, detail, create, update, and remove operations for tour categories, delegating to TourCategoriesService.

nodes.name: TourCategoriesController
nodes.summary: Public controller serving category list and detail-by-slug queries to unauthenticated clients.

nodes.name: TourCategoriesService
nodes.summary: Service encapsulating tour-category queries and mutations over Prisma, including shared pagination, slug normalization, and error-to-exception mapping.

nodes.name: admin-cancellations.controller.ts
nodes.summary: Admin-only REST controller exposing endpoints to list booking cancellation requests and deny a specific request. Delegates to CancellationsService and is guarded by role decorators.

nodes.name: cancellations.controller.ts
nodes.summary: Customer-facing REST controller that lets the booking owner submit a cancellation request for a PAID booking, delegating to CancellationsService.

nodes.name: cancellations.module.ts
nodes.summary: NestJS module that wires the customer and admin cancellation controllers together with CancellationsService.

nodes.name: cancellations.service.ts
nodes.summary: Core domain service for booking cancellations: creates owner-only cancellation requests (with PAID/departure/open-request guards), lists them for admins, and denies requests, enqueuing outbox notification rows within Prisma transactions.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses Prisma $transaction for atomic upsert-plus-outbox writes and race-safe upsert/updateMany guards on the CancellationRequest model.

nodes.name: cancellation-request.dto.ts
nodes.summary: Response DTOs for cancellation requests: the customer-facing summary, the admin request view with booking reference, and the paginated admin list envelope.

nodes.name: create-cancellation-request.dto.ts
nodes.summary: Request DTO for submitting a booking cancellation, carrying an optional customer reason.

nodes.name: deny-cancellation-request.dto.ts
nodes.summary: Request DTO for an admin denying a cancellation request, carrying an optional decision note.

nodes.name: list-cancellation-requests-query.dto.ts
nodes.summary: Query DTO for the admin cancellation-requests list, supporting pagination and status filtering.

nodes.name: AdminCancellationsController
nodes.summary: Admin controller with list and deny handlers over cancellation requests, delegating to CancellationsService.

nodes.name: CancellationsController
nodes.summary: Customer controller whose request handler forwards a booking cancellation submission to CancellationsService for the current user.

nodes.name: CancellationsService
nodes.summary: Injectable service implementing createRequest, findAllForAdmin, and denyRequest, enforcing ownership and state guards and persisting request state plus outbox notifications atomically.

nodes.name: AdminCancellationRequestDto
nodes.summary: Admin-facing DTO describing a cancellation request with its status, timestamps, decision note, and referenced booking details.

nodes.name: admin-departures.controller.ts
nodes.summary: Admin-only REST controller exposing tour-departure management (list/create/update/remove) under a role-guarded route, delegating all logic to DeparturesService.

nodes.name: departures.controller.ts
nodes.summary: Public REST controller returning the upcoming departure schedule for a published tour by slug.

nodes.name: departures.module.ts
nodes.summary: NestJS module wiring the public and admin departure controllers to the shared DeparturesService.

nodes.name: departures.service.ts
nodes.summary: Core departures business logic: public/admin listing with date filters, plus create/update/remove of tour departures with date-range validation, past-date guards, and Prisma persistence.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses Prisma Decimal conversion helpers and foreign-key error detection to translate DB constraint errors into HTTP responses.

nodes.name: create-departure.dto.ts
nodes.summary: Validated request DTO for creating a tour departure (dates, seat count, optional price overrides, status).

nodes.name: departure.dto.ts
nodes.summary: Response DTO describing the shape of a tour departure returned to clients (ids, dates, pricing, seat availability, status, timestamps).

nodes.name: list-departures-query.dto.ts
nodes.summary: Query-string DTO for filtering departure listings by date range (from/to) and status.

nodes.name: update-departure.dto.ts
nodes.summary: Partial-update DTO for departures, derived from CreateDepartureDto via PartialType.

nodes.name: AdminDeparturesController
nodes.summary: Role-guarded admin controller with list/create/update/remove endpoints that proxy to DeparturesService for departure management.

nodes.name: DeparturesController
nodes.summary: Public controller exposing a single endpoint to list a published tour's departures.

nodes.name: DeparturesService
nodes.summary: Injectable service holding all departure logic: public/admin listings with date filtering, create/update/remove with validation and past-date guards, and Prisma-backed persistence.

nodes.name: CreateDepartureDto
nodes.summary: Validated payload for creating a departure with start/end dates, seat total, optional price overrides, and status.

nodes.name: DepartureDto
nodes.summary: Serialized representation of a departure returned by the API, including pricing and seat availability.

nodes.name: admin-enquiry.controller.ts
nodes.summary: Admin-only REST controller exposing endpoints to list tour enquiries with pagination/filters and update an enquiry's status.
nodes.languageNotes: NestJS controller guarded by role-based access via the Roles decorator.

nodes.name: create-enquiry.dto.ts
nodes.summary: Request DTO validating incoming enquiry submissions, including contact details, tour reference, trip preferences, and a honeypot field.
nodes.languageNotes: class-validator decorators enforce field constraints; the 'website' honeypot property guards against spam bots.

nodes.name: enquiry.dto.ts
nodes.summary: Response DTOs for the enquiry module: submission acknowledgement, the full enquiry record shape, and a paginated list envelope.

nodes.name: list-enquiries-query.dto.ts
nodes.summary: Query DTO for the admin enquiry listing endpoint, carrying pagination, status filter, and free-text search parameters.

nodes.name: update-enquiry-status.dto.ts
nodes.summary: Minimal request DTO validating the new status value when an admin updates an enquiry.

nodes.name: enquiry.controller.ts
nodes.summary: Public REST controller accepting anonymous enquiry submissions, applying honeypot spam filtering before delegating to the service.
nodes.languageNotes: Marked with the Public decorator to bypass auth guards for anonymous submissions.

nodes.name: enquiry.module.ts
nodes.summary: NestJS module wiring the public and admin enquiry controllers together with the enquiry service.

nodes.name: enquiry.service.ts
nodes.summary: Core enquiry business logic: persists submissions transactionally with an outbox notification event, lists enquiries for admins with search/pagination, and updates enquiry status.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses Prisma $transaction to atomically create the enquiry and an outbox record for async notification dispatch.

nodes.name: AdminEnquiryController
nodes.summary: Handles admin enquiry endpoints: listing enquiries with query filters and updating an enquiry's status.

nodes.name: EnquiryController
nodes.summary: Exposes the public POST endpoint for submitting an enquiry, short-circuiting when the honeypot field is filled.

nodes.name: EnquiryService
nodes.summary: Service encapsulating enquiry creation with transactional outbox events, admin listing with search/pagination, and status updates.

nodes.name: CreateEnquiryDto
nodes.summary: Validated payload for creating an enquiry, covering contact info, tour reference, trip preferences, and a spam honeypot field.

nodes.name: EnquiryDto
nodes.summary: Serialized representation of an enquiry record returned to clients, including linked tour metadata and status.

nodes.name: ListEnquiriesQueryDto
nodes.summary: Validated query parameters for the admin enquiry listing endpoint: page, pageSize, status, and search.

nodes.name: insert.ts
nodes.summary: Inserts the committed sample fixtures into the database in foreign-key-safe order using createMany with skipDuplicates. Shared by the standalone load-fixtures runner and prisma/seed.ts.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses `as any` casts to keep readonly `as const` fixtures assignable to Prisma's CreateMany input types; coerces date-only @db.Date strings to Date to satisfy Prisma 7.

nodes.name: load-fixtures.ts
nodes.summary: Standalone runner that opens a PrismaPg connection and delegates to insertFixtures to load the sample dataset, guarded against accidentally writing to a non-local (production) database.
nodes.languageNotes: Local-DB safety guard refuses to run against non-localhost connection strings unless LOAD_FIXTURES_CONFIRM=1 is set.

nodes.name: sample-data.ts
nodes.summary: Large committed fixture dataset exporting typed arrays for all 18 domain models (users, tours, destinations, bookings, reviews, posts, media, etc.) used to seed the tourism catalog and sample transactional data.
nodes.languageNotes: ~369 rows across 19 `as const` exports serving as the single source of truth for seeding and the test suite.

nodes.name: seed.ts
nodes.summary: Prisma database seed that loads the sample fixtures then overlays login-able CUSTOMER/ADMIN accounts and one self-signed PAID booking so the review flow, my-bookings, and e2e suite run without a live payment.
nodes.languageNotes: Idempotent via createMany skipDuplicates + upserts; claims seats atomically inside a $transaction and writes seeded identifiers to a gitignored .env.e2e.

nodes.name: insertFixtures
nodes.summary: Runs an ordered list of createMany insert steps (parents before children) for every model, coercing @db.Date fields and logging per-table row counts, returning the total rows inserted.

nodes.name: pickPaidDeparture
nodes.summary: Selects an OPEN departure on a published tour with enough free seats for the self-signed PAID booking, preferring a configured tour slug else the soonest qualifying departure.

nodes.name: main
nodes.summary: Seed entry point: inserts fixtures, upserts login-able customer and admin users, creates one self-signed PAID booking with atomic seat claim, and writes e2e identifiers to .env.e2e.

nodes.name: .env.example
nodes.summary: Template of the 30 runtime environment variables the NestJS API needs: server/app settings, Supabase Postgres + auth, Cloudinary media, Stripe and PayPal payments, Resend email, Sentry, and throttling. Copied to .env for local development.
nodes.languageNotes: Groups secrets by integration (Supabase, Stripe, PayPal, Cloudinary, Resend, Sentry); values are placeholders validated at boot by env.validation.ts.

nodes.name: package.json
nodes.summary: Package manifest for the @tourism/api NestJS app, declaring runtime and dev dependencies plus embedded Nx target overrides (e.g. a seed target running the Prisma seed script).
nodes.languageNotes: Carries an inline `nx` block with custom executors/targets rather than a separate project.json.

nodes.name: tsconfig.app.json
nodes.summary: TypeScript build configuration for the application source, extending the base tsconfig and excluding spec files so production compilation covers only runtime code.

nodes.name: tsconfig.json
nodes.summary: Root TypeScript configuration for the API project that extends the workspace base config and wires project references to the app and spec tsconfigs.

nodes.name: tsconfig.spec.json
nodes.summary: TypeScript configuration for the Jest/test build, including spec and test files with test-specific compiler options.

nodes.name: bookings.json
nodes.summary: Seed fixture of 30 booking records (code, amounts, status, payment provider, contact + refund fields) linking users, tours, and departures for the database seed loader.

nodes.name: destinations.json
nodes.summary: Seed fixture of 16 destination records (slug, name, country, region, description) used to populate the destinations catalog.

nodes.name: enquiries.json
nodes.summary: Seed fixture of 25 enquiry/contact records (name, email, message, travel intent, interests, status) optionally tied to a tour.

nodes.name: mediaAssets.json
nodes.summary: Seed fixture of 83 polymorphic media asset records (Cloudinary publicId, type, owner, role, dimensions) attached to tours, destinations, and posts.

nodes.name: mediaGarbage.json
nodes.summary: Seed fixture of 4 orphaned media-garbage records (publicId, attempts, lastError) representing pending Cloudinary cleanup entries.

nodes.name: outbox.json
nodes.summary: Seed fixture of 12 transactional-outbox records (type, payload, status, attempts, dedupeKey) modeling queued async jobs for the pg-boss worker.

nodes.name: paymentEvents.json
nodes.summary: Seed fixture of 18 payment webhook event records (provider, eventId, type, payload) capturing Stripe/PayPal idempotency events.

nodes.name: posts.json
nodes.summary: Seed fixture of 10 blog post records (slug, title, excerpt, markdown content, status, author) powering the blog reader.

nodes.name: reviews.json
nodes.summary: Seed fixture of 26 tour review records (rating, title, body, author, approval flags) linked to tours, users, and bookings.

nodes.name: tourCategories.json
nodes.summary: Seed fixture of 6 tour category records (slug, name, description, order) used to classify tours.

nodes.name: tourDepartures.json
nodes.summary: Seed fixture of 50 tour departure records (start/end dates, price overrides, seats total/booked, status) providing scheduled availability per tour.

nodes.name: tourDestinations.json
nodes.summary: Seed fixture of 38 tour-destination join records (tourId, destinationId, isPrimary) mapping the many-to-many relation between tours and destinations.

nodes.name: tourFaqs.json
nodes.summary: Seed fixture of 13 tour FAQ records (question, answer, order) attached to individual tours.

nodes.name: tourItineraryDays.json
nodes.summary: Seed fixture of 15 tour itinerary-day records (dayNumber, title, description) describing the day-by-day plan for tours.

nodes.name: tourPolicies.json
nodes.summary: Seed fixture of 16 tour policy records (kind, title, body, order) covering cancellation and booking policy content per tour.

nodes.name: tours.json
nodes.summary: Seed fixture of 23 tour records (slug, title, pricing, difficulty, badges, inclusions, highlights) forming the core product catalog.

nodes.name: users.json
nodes.summary: Seed fixture of 24 user records (supabaseId, email, fullName, role) mirroring Supabase-authenticated accounts locally.

nodes.name: wishlist.json
nodes.summary: Seed fixture of 20 wishlist join records (userId, tourId, createdAt) capturing saved-tour relations between users and tours.

nodes.name: .spec.swcrc
nodes.summary: SWC transform configuration used to compile TypeScript spec files fast for the Jest unit-test run.

nodes.name: .understandignore
nodes.summary: Ignore-list controlling which paths the Understand-Anything scanner skips when building the knowledge graph.

nodes.name: eslint.config.mjs
nodes.summary: Project-level ESLint flat config that re-exports the shared workspace lint configuration for the API app.

nodes.name: jest.config.cts
nodes.summary: Jest configuration for the API unit-test suite (SWC transform, ts-node roots, coverage settings).

nodes.name: jest.config.e2e.cts
nodes.summary: Jest configuration dedicated to the API end-to-end test suite, isolating e2e specs from unit runs.

nodes.name: prisma.config.ts
nodes.summary: Prisma CLI configuration wiring the schema path, migrations, and seed command for the API's Postgres database.

nodes.name: gen.cjs
nodes.summary: Deterministic fixture generator that builds the full sample dataset (429 records across 18 models) consumed by the seed loader, exercising edge cases across bookings, media, enquiries, and outbox.
nodes.languageNotes: Single source-of-truth generator whose output feeds both sample-data.ts and the JSON fixtures; helper functions build referentially-consistent rows via id/foreign-key helpers.

nodes.name: addDep
nodes.summary: Builds a tour-departure fixture row with derived pricing, seat capacity, and status.

nodes.name: addBooking
nodes.summary: Constructs a booking fixture including derived code, provider/session fields, and status-dependent amounts.

nodes.name: addEvent
nodes.summary: Adds a payment-event fixture row tied to a booking and provider.

nodes.name: addEnquiry
nodes.summary: Builds an enquiry/lead fixture row with contact and tour-reference fields.

nodes.name: addOutbox
nodes.summary: Creates an outbox fixture entry with type, payload, dedupe key, and status.

nodes.name: addMedia
nodes.summary: Builds a media-asset fixture row with owner-type/owner-id, role, and public id.

nodes.name: chkEnum
nodes.summary: Validates that a generated value belongs to an allowed enum set, guarding fixture integrity.

nodes.name: reset.ts
nodes.summary: Database reset script that TRUNCATEs every application table (CASCADE, restart identity) for from-zero manual testing, preserving schema, RLS, pg-boss, and Supabase Auth accounts.

nodes.name: README.md
nodes.summary: Documents the sample fixture dataset — the 429 records across 18 models, deliberately exercised edge cases, and authoring conventions for the generator.

nodes.name: migration_lock.toml
nodes.summary: Prisma migration lock file pinning the migration provider to PostgreSQL.

nodes.name: hardening.sql
nodes.summary: Reference-only hardening DDL that Prisma's schema cannot express (ADR-0008): citext email extension, CHECK constraints, and row-level-security policies across the 15 app tables.
nodes.languageNotes: Now reference-only — the applied source of truth is the 20260615071228_hardening migration; RLS on _prisma_migrations is applied separately via a Supabase migration.

nodes.name: 20260615071041_init
nodes.summary: Initial database migration creating the full core schema: 15 tables (users, tours, destinations, bookings, reviews, media, etc.) plus enums and indexes.

nodes.name: users
nodes.summary: User table mirroring Supabase Auth accounts locally, with role, email, locale, and profile fields.

nodes.name: tour_categories
nodes.summary: Tour category taxonomy (slug, name, ordering, active flag) for classifying tours.

nodes.name: destinations
nodes.summary: Destination catalog with slug, country, region, and description used to group and market tours.

nodes.name: tours
nodes.summary: Core tours table holding product details, publishing/featured flags, category link, and merchandising fields.

nodes.name: tour_destinations
nodes.summary: Join table linking tours to their destinations, with a primary-destination flag.

nodes.name: tour_itinerary_days
nodes.summary: Per-day itinerary entries for a tour, keyed by tour and day number.

nodes.name: tour_departures
nodes.summary: Scheduled tour departures with start date, pricing, seat capacity, and availability status.

nodes.name: tour_faqs
nodes.summary: Ordered FAQ entries attached to a tour.

nodes.name: tour_policies
nodes.summary: Tour policy entries (by kind) describing terms such as cancellation and inclusions.

nodes.name: bookings
nodes.summary: Bookings table with unique code, departure/user links, payment provider/session, status, and amounts — the money-path core.

nodes.name: reviews
nodes.summary: Reviews tied to bookings/tours with approval and featured flags.

nodes.name: wishlist
nodes.summary: Wishlist join rows linking users to saved tours.

nodes.name: payment_events
nodes.summary: Payment provider webhook/event log with unique provider event id for idempotency.

nodes.name: enquiries
nodes.summary: Customer enquiry/contact submissions with status tracking.

nodes.name: media_assets
nodes.summary: Polymorphic media asset table (owner type/id + role) referencing Cloudinary public ids.

nodes.name: 20260615071228_hardening
nodes.summary: Hardening migration applying the citext extension, CHECK constraints, and row-level-security policies across the app tables (ADR-0008).

nodes.name: 20260616000000_rename_paymentprovider_momo_to_paypal
nodes.summary: Renames the PaymentProvider enum value MOMO to PAYPAL (ADR-0006 amendment) as PayPal replaces MoMo as the second payment rail.

nodes.name: 20260620000000_p1x_outbox
nodes.summary: Adds the transactional outbox table with dedupe key and status/created-at index for reliable async job dispatch.

nodes.name: outbox
nodes.summary: Transactional outbox rows (type, payload, status, attempts, dedupe key) for reliable async processing.

nodes.name: 20260620010000_p1xb_media_garbage
nodes.summary: Adds the media_garbage table tracking orphaned Cloudinary assets pending deletion (GC-on-delete).

nodes.name: media_garbage
nodes.summary: Queue of orphaned media public ids awaiting remote deletion, with attempt and error tracking.

nodes.name: 20260620020000_p17d_enquiry_lead_fields
nodes.summary: Adds lead-capture columns to the enquiries table.

nodes.name: 20260620030000_p17e_tour_merchandising
nodes.summary: Adds merchandising columns to the tours table (e.g. compare-at pricing / featured merchandising fields).

nodes.name: 20260621124125_p_content_posts
nodes.summary: Adds the posts table backing the blog, with slug, status, published-at, and author relation plus supporting indexes.

nodes.name: posts
nodes.summary: Blog posts table with slug, title, excerpt, markdown content, publish status, and author link.

nodes.name: 20260628000000_unified_reviews
nodes.summary: Migration unifying the reviews model and adding an approved/featured composite index.

nodes.name: 20260702142704_add_tour_id_indexes
nodes.summary: Adds tour_id indexes on bookings and enquiries to speed up tour-scoped lookups.

nodes.name: 20260703120425_add_post_tags_and_post_tours
nodes.summary: Adds blog-v2 relations: post_tags plus post_tag_links and post_tours join tables with supporting indexes.

nodes.name: post_tags
nodes.summary: Blog tag vocabulary (slug, name) for classifying posts.

nodes.name: post_tag_links
nodes.summary: Join table linking posts to their tags.

nodes.name: post_tours
nodes.summary: Ordered join table relating blog posts to featured tours.

nodes.name: 20260703144308_add_media_role_body
nodes.summary: Adds the body value to the MediaRole enum to support inline blog body images.

nodes.name: 20260705012606_add_subscribers_and_media_asset_unique
nodes.summary: Adds the newsletter subscribers table and a unique constraint on media assets (owner + public id) for race-free upserts.

nodes.name: subscribers
nodes.summary: Newsletter subscribers table (unique email, source, subscribed-at).

nodes.name: 20260705015607_refund_cancellation_queue
nodes.summary: Adds refund execution support (partial-refund fields/status) and the cancellation_requests queue table with status index.

nodes.name: cancellation_requests
nodes.summary: Booking cancellation-request queue with reason, status, and admin decision fields.

nodes.name: schema.prisma
nodes.summary: Prisma data model for the entire tourism platform, defining 25 models (User, Tour, Destination, Booking, Review, Post, MediaAsset, Outbox, CancellationRequest, Subscriber, etc.) and 15 enums covering booking, payment, media, and content workflows.
nodes.languageNotes: Single Prisma schema is the source of truth for the Postgres/Supabase database; models map to booking, catalog, payments, reviews, blog, media, and job-outbox domains with rich relations and enum-driven state machines.

nodes.name: .gitkeep
nodes.summary: Empty placeholder file that keeps the otherwise-empty assets directory tracked in git.

nodes.name: index.ts
nodes.summary: Barrel module re-exporting shared cross-cutting primitives: the response TransformInterceptor, HttpExceptionFilter, Supabase JWT and roles guards, and the Public/Roles/CurrentUser decorators plus auth types.

nodes.name: configuration.ts
nodes.summary: Defines namespaced, typed NestJS configuration factories (app, supabase, cloudinary, stripe, paypal, email, sentry, throttler) read from validated env vars, aggregated into a configurations array for ConfigModule.forRoot.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses @nestjs/config registerAs to create dotted-path config namespaces injected via ConfigService.

nodes.name: env.validation.ts
nodes.summary: Exports the environment-variable validation schema that fails fast at boot when required configuration (database, Supabase, payment provider, and email keys) is missing or malformed.

nodes.name: webpack.config.js
nodes.summary: Webpack build configuration for the NestJS API, wiring the Nx Node build plugin and output/asset settings for the server bundle.

layers.name: HTTP Controllers Layer
layers.description: NestJS controllers that expose the REST API surface — route handlers per feature module (tours, bookings, payments, cancellations, reviews, posts, media, newsletter, admin-stats) mapping HTTP requests to domain services.

layers.name: Domain Service Layer
layers.description: Business logic and orchestration — feature services, NestJS module wiring, pg-boss job processors, email/outbox senders, and domain helpers (booking-code, refund-amount, money, next-departure) implementing tours, bookings, Stripe/PayPal payments and refunds.

layers.name: API Contracts & DTOs
layers.description: Request/response DTOs, input objects, enums and shared type definitions that define and validate the API contract (Swagger/class-validator) across every feature module.

layers.name: Cross-Cutting Concerns
layers.description: Framework-wide infrastructure in common/ and lib/ — response-envelope interceptor, exception filter, RolesGuard and SupabaseJwtGuard auth, parameter decorators, and slugify utilities.

layers.name: Data & Persistence Layer
layers.description: Prisma schema (25 models/15 enums), SQL migrations and table definitions, RLS hardening, PrismaService, plus the seed/reset scripts and fixture generator that populate the database.

layers.name: Application Bootstrap
layers.description: Composition root — main.ts bootstrap (Nest factory, Swagger, Sentry, global pipes) and the root AppModule/health controller that wires every feature module together.

layers.name: Configuration & Build Tooling
layers.description: Namespaced config factories with env validation plus root build/test tooling — TypeScript, ESLint, Jest, webpack, SWC, package manifest, static assets and the e2e harness.

tour.title: Application Bootstrap
tour.description: Every request into this NestJS API begins here. `main.ts` is the composition root: it initializes Sentry, creates the Nest application, wires the Stripe raw-body middleware (needed for webhook signature verification later), and applies Helmet, CORS, the global validation pipe, and Swagger docs before starting the HTTP listener. The companion `.env.example` catalogs the ~30 runtime variables the server needs — Supabase Postgres, Stripe/PayPal, Resend email, Cloudinary, and Sentry — giving you a one-glance map of every external system this API talks to.
tour.languageLesson: A NestJS app is bootstrapped by `NestFactory.create(AppModule)`. Global cross-cutting behavior (pipes, filters, interceptors, middleware) is registered here at the composition root rather than per-controller, so it applies uniformly to every route.

tour.title: Root Module Wiring
tour.description: `app.module.ts` is the highest fan-out file in the codebase — it imports every feature module (auth, bookings, tours, payments, jobs, newsletter, and more) and registers the global guards, filters, and interceptors as providers. Building on the bootstrap from Step 1, this is where Nest's dependency-injection graph is actually assembled. The `config/index.ts` barrel supplies the environment-validation schema that the ConfigModule enforces at startup, so a misconfigured deployment fails fast instead of at first request.
tour.languageLesson: NestJS modules are the unit of composition. `APP_GUARD`, `APP_FILTER`, and `APP_INTERCEPTOR` provider tokens let a module register cross-cutting components globally through DI, which is how the auth guard and response envelope in the next steps become app-wide.

tour.title: The Response Envelope & Error Handling
tour.description: These two global components shape every HTTP response the API returns. The `TransformInterceptor` wraps successful controller results in a standard success envelope (unless a handler opts out with `@SkipTransform`), while the `HttpExceptionFilter` normalizes any thrown error into a matching JSON error envelope, mapping HTTP status to stable error codes and forwarding 5xx failures to Sentry. Understanding this contract early means every later endpoint's output shape is predictable.
tour.languageLesson: In NestJS the request pipeline is: middleware → guards → interceptors (pre) → handler → interceptors (post) → exception filters. Interceptors transform successful responses; filters catch thrown exceptions. Together they guarantee a single, consistent envelope regardless of which controller ran.

tour.title: Authentication & Authorization
tour.description: This is the security gate standing in front of every route. The global `SupabaseJwtGuard` verifies incoming Supabase JWTs (remote JWKS for RS256, shared secret for HS256), skips routes marked `@Public()`, and attaches the mirrored local user to the request. The `RolesGuard` then reads `@Roles` metadata to enforce role-based access, and the `@CurrentUser` parameter decorator hands the authenticated user straight to handlers. This decorator-plus-guard pattern is used pervasively — note the high fan-in on `roles.decorator.ts`.
tour.languageLesson: NestJS guards use the `Reflector` to read metadata set by custom decorators (`@Roles`, `@Public`) on handlers. This keeps authorization declarative: the rule lives next to the route as an annotation, and the guard resolves it at request time.

tour.title: Data Access with Prisma
tour.description: `PrismaService` is the most depended-upon file in the entire API (fan-in of 25) — nearly every feature service injects it to reach the database. It wraps `PrismaClient` with the `PrismaPg` driver adapter and manages the connection lifecycle, connecting on module init and disconnecting on destroy. The driver adapter is a deliberate choice: the Supabase transaction pooler can't start batch `$transaction` calls under concurrency, so parallel reads use `Promise.all` instead. This service is the single seam between the domain layer and Postgres.

tour.title: The Booking Request Enters
tour.description: Now we follow a real request through the richest feature in the app. `BookingsController` is the customer-facing surface for the whole booking lifecycle: create, list-own, cancel-pending, start-checkout, capture-PayPal, and view-detail. The `CreateBookingDto` defines the request contract with class-validator rules — tour slug, departure, party sizes, chosen payment provider, and contact details — which the global validation pipe from Step 1 enforces before the handler ever runs. This is where the money path begins.
tour.languageLesson: class-validator decorators on a DTO (`@IsString`, `@IsInt`, `@IsEnum`) turn the request body into a validated, typed object automatically. Invalid input is rejected with a 400 before controller code executes, so handlers can trust their inputs.

tour.title: Booking Domain Logic
tour.description: `BookingsService` is the heart of the money path. Building on the controller from Step 6, it creates bookings with seat-availability and pricing checks, initiates Stripe or PayPal checkout, captures PayPal orders, cancels pending bookings, and executes admin refunds. The pure helper `booking-code.ts` mints a human-readable, collision-resistant reference from cryptographic random bytes — the customer-visible code that threads through payments, emails, and cancellations. Notice how business rules live here in the service, keeping the controller thin.

tour.title: Payment Provider Integrations
tour.description: When the booking service starts checkout, it delegates to these provider adapters. `StripeService` lazily initializes the SDK, creates Checkout sessions, verifies webhook signatures, and issues refunds; `PaypalService` creates and captures orders, refunds captures, verifies webhook signatures, and caches OAuth access tokens. Both hide provider-specific detail behind a uniform interface so the booking service stays payment-agnostic. The user is redirected to a hosted checkout page here, and the flow pauses until the provider calls back.
tour.languageLesson: External payment SDKs are wrapped in dedicated services so provider quirks (Stripe's raw-body signature check, PayPal's OAuth token caching) stay isolated. This adapter pattern lets the domain layer switch providers by a single enum on the booking without touching business logic.

tour.title: Webhooks & Payment Reconciliation
tour.description: This is where the money path closes the loop. `PaymentsController` is a public webhook endpoint that receives the raw Stripe and PayPal payloads (recall the raw-body middleware wired in Step 1) and forwards them to `PaymentsService`. That service idempotently records each payment event and reconciles the outcome — claiming the reserved seats on success, or refunding an overbooked booking when seats are no longer available. Idempotency is critical here because providers retry webhooks, and the same event must never double-charge or double-claim.
tour.languageLesson: Payment webhooks are untrusted and delivered at-least-once. The defense is signature verification (proving the sender) plus idempotent event recording (a unique event id), so replays and retries converge to the same final state instead of applying an effect twice.

tour.title: Async Jobs & the Transactional Outbox
tour.description: A successful payment doesn't send the confirmation email inline — it writes an outbox row in the same transaction that claims the seats. `JobsService` runs pg-boss: it starts the queue, registers workers, and schedules the outbox drain, abandoned-booking cancellation, and media-reconciliation cron jobs. `OutboxService` drains pending rows and dispatches each via `EmailService` (Resend-backed), recording success or bounded-retry failure. The pure `email.templates.ts` renders the subject/text/html bodies. This decoupling means email delivery can't roll back a paid booking.
tour.languageLesson: The transactional-outbox pattern writes the intent-to-send-email into the database inside the same transaction as the state change, then a background worker delivers it. This guarantees the side effect happens exactly when the business fact is committed — no lost emails, no emails for rolled-back bookings.

tour.title: Refunds & Cancellations
tour.description: Closing the lifecycle: when a booking is cancelled or refunded, the pure helper `refund-amount.ts` classifies the requested amount against the booking total as full or partial using Decimal comparison (avoiding floating-point money bugs), and `RefundBookingDto` validates the admin's optional partial-amount input. These feed the refund execution path in the booking service from Step 7, which calls back into the provider services from Step 8 with an idempotency key. This is the reverse of the money path — money flowing back out safely.
tour.languageLesson: Monetary amounts use arbitrary-precision Decimal rather than JS floating-point `number`, because 0.1 + 0.2 !== 0.3 in IEEE-754. Comparing refund-vs-total with Decimal prevents off-by-a-cent errors that would misclassify a full refund as partial.

tour.title: The Data Model & Migrations
tour.description: Finally, the foundation everything above rests on. `schema.prisma` defines all 25 models and 15 enums — User, Tour, Booking, Payment, Outbox, CancellationRequest, and more — the vocabulary the whole codebase speaks. The initial migration created the core tables, with the `bookings` table being the money-path core (unique code, departure/user links, provider/session, status, and amounts). Because Prisma can't express certain guarantees, `hardening.sql` adds citext email, CHECK constraints, and row-level-security policies across all app tables as a reference-only DDL layer.
tour.languageLesson: Prisma migrations are ordered, incremental, and forward-only in production. Constraints Prisma's schema language can't express (row-level security, CHECK constraints, Postgres extensions) live in raw SQL alongside the generated migrations — a common escape hatch for database-level guarantees.
